reason: The remediation involves modifying PAM (Pluggable Authentication Modules) configuration via authselect or direct file editing. While this is a standard hardening pattern, the remediation script explicitly warns that if authselect integrity checks fail, the process must be aborted. Incorrect PAM configuration can lead to immediate lockout from the system (SSH/Console).
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential for system lockout if PAM configuration is corrupted; Dependency on authselect profile integrity; Manual editing of /etc/pam.d/ files is risky
